commands.review_summary:
The PR adds standalone debug proxy header redaction, unit coverage for `redactHeaders`, and an Unreleased changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main writes raw standalone request, response, and CONNECT headers into persisted capture events while the runtime path already redacts them. I did not run a live proxy capture because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor/maintainer review because the branch has code blockers and contributor-side real behavior proof is still required; this should not be routed to a ClawSweeper repair lane while proof is insufficient.

target.title: fix(proxy): redact sensitive headers in standalone debug proxy captures
target.body:
## Problem

The standalone debug proxy (`openclaw proxy start`) stores raw request and response headers in capture events via `JSON.stringify(req.headers)` without redaction. This includes `Authorization`, `x-api-key`, `Cookie`, and other sensitive headers in the capture database.

The runtime proxy path (`runtime.ts`) already uses `redactedCaptureHeaders()` with a comprehensive sensitive-header allowlist (lines 19-41), but the standalone `proxy-server.ts` does not. This inconsistency means captured traffic from `openclaw proxy start` contains secrets that `openclaw proxy run` would redact.

## Fix

Add a `redactHeaders` function to `proxy-server.ts` that mirrors the `runtime.ts` sensitive header list and apply it to all three header storage points:
1. HTTP request headers (line 208)
2. HTTP response headers (line 240)
3. CONNECT request headers (line 309)

The sensitive header list matches `runtime.ts` exactly: exact names (`authorization`, `proxy-authorization`, `cookie`, `set-cookie`, `x-api-key`, `api-key`, `apikey`, `x-auth-token`, `auth-token`, `x-access-token`, `access-token`) plus fragment matching for `api-key`, `apikey`, `token`, `secret`, `password`, `credential`, `session`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: sensitive headers (Authorization, API keys, tokens, cookies, session IDs) are now redacted in standalone debug proxy capture events, matching the existing runtime proxy redaction. A missing `session` fragment was added in a follow-up commit to close the parity gap.
Real environment tested: local source checkout of openclaw/openclaw at commit 684a6303b3 (current upstream/main) on Linux x86_64, Node v24.1.0, pnpm 11.1.0.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the patch, ran `pnpm tsgo:core` for type check, then exercised the redaction function inline to verify sensitive headers are replaced with `[REDACTED]` while safe headers pass through.
Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

Before (on main, raw headers stored verbatim in capture DB):
```console
$ node -e "
const testHeaders = {
  'Host': 'api.example.com',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Authorization': 'Bearer sk-secret-key-123',
  'X-Api-Key': 'key-abc-456',
  'Cookie': 'session_token=xyz789',
  'X-Session-Id': 'sess_live_abcdef',
  'X-Custom-Session-Token': 'tok_123',
};
console.log(JSON.stringify(testHeaders, null, 2));
"
{
  "Host": "api.example.com",
  "Content-Type": "application/json",
  "Authorization": "Bearer sk-secret-key-123",
  "X-Api-Key": "key-abc-456",
  "Cookie": "session_token=xyz789",
  "X-Session-Id": "sess_live_abcdef",
  "X-Custom-Session-Token": "tok_123"
}
```

After (with this patch, `redactHeaders()` applied before `JSON.stringify`):
```console
$ node -e "
const SENSITIVE_HEADER_NAMES = new Set([
  'authorization', 'proxy-authorization', 'cookie', 'set-cookie',
  'x-api-key', 'api-key', 'apikey', 'x-auth-token', 'auth-token',
  'x-access-token', 'access-token',
]);
const SENSITIVE_HEADER_FRAGMENTS = [
  'api-key', 'apikey', 'token', 'secret', 'password', 'credential', 'session',
];
function redactHeaders(headers) {
  const redacted = {};
  for (const [name, value] of Object.entries(headers)) {
    const lower = name.toLowerCase();
    const isSensitive =
      SENSITIVE_HEADER_NAMES.has(lower) ||
      SENSITIVE_HEADER_FRAGMENTS.some((f) => lower.includes(f));
    redacted[name] = isSensitive ? '[REDACTED]' : value;
  }
  return redacted;
}
const testHeaders = {
  'Host': 'api.example.com',
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Authorization': 'Bearer sk-secret-key-123',
  'X-Api-Key': 'key-abc-456',
  'Cookie': 'session_token=xyz789',
  'X-Session-Id': 'sess_live_abcdef',
  'X-Custom-Session-Token': 'tok_123',
  'X-Request-Id': 'req-42',
  'User-Agent': 'openclaw/2026.5',
  'X-Secret-Header': 'very-private',
  'X-Credential-Id': 'cred-99',
};
console.log(JSON.stringify(redactHeaders(testHeaders), null, 2));
"
{
  "Host": "api.example.com",
  "Content-Type": "application/json",
  "Authorization": "[REDACTED]",
  "X-Api-Key": "[REDACTED]",
  "Cookie": "[REDACTED]",
  "X-Session-Id": "[REDACTED]",
  "X-Custom-Session-Token": "[REDACTED]",
  "X-Request-Id": "req-42",
  "User-Agent": "openclaw/2026.5",
  "X-Secret-Header": "[REDACTED]",
  "X-Credential-Id": "[REDACTED]"
}
```

Fragment list parity check (standalone vs runtime):
```console
$ echo "=== Standalone (proxy-server.ts) ===" && grep -A10 'SENSITIVE_HEADER_FRAGMENTS' src/proxy-capture/proxy-server.ts | head -10
=== Standalone (proxy-server.ts) ===
const SENSITIVE_HEADER_FRAGMENTS = [
  "api-key",
  "apikey",
  "token",
  "secret",
  "password",
  "credential",
  "session",
];

$ echo "=== Runtime (runtime.ts) ===" && grep -A10 'SENSITIVE_CAPTURE_HEADER_NAME_FRAGMENTS' src/proxy-capture/runtime.ts | head -10
=== Runtime (runtime.ts) ===
const SENSITIVE_CAPTURE_HEADER_NAME_FRAGMENTS = [
  "api-key",
  "apikey",
  "token",
  "secret",
  "password",
  "credential",
  "session",
];
```

Type check:
```console
$ pnpm tsgo:core
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
```
No errors.

All header storage points use redactHeaders():
```console
$ grep -n 'headersJson' src/proxy-capture/proxy-server.ts
208:      headersJson: JSON.stringify(redactHeaders(req.headers)),
240:            redactHeaders(upstreamRes.headers as Record<string, string | string[] | undefined>),
309:      headersJson: JSON.stringify(redactHeaders(req.headers)),
```

Observed result after fix: all three header storage points now use `redactHeaders()`. Authorization, API keys, cookies, session IDs, secrets, credentials, and token headers are all replaced with `[REDACTED]`. Safe headers (Host, Content-Type, User-Agent, X-Request-Id) pass through. The standalone fragment list now includes `session`, matching the runtime path exactly.
What was not tested: live proxy capture with traffic to a real API and inspection of the SQLite capture database. The redaction logic is exercised inline above; the runtime path has integration coverage in `runtime.test.ts`.
